verdict: Our final temporal AUROC is higher than P1 postdevelopment but protocols differ; both support drift sensitivity.

paper_protocol: Coswara internal cough audio, calibrated DNN
our_metric: AUROC 0.897, AUPRC 0.863, ECE 0.170 for final selected row
verdict: Our AUROC higher, but ENCL calibration is better; not identical features/splits.
Metric differs (accuracy vs AUROC) but modality/feature family are close; our speech result is moderate, not SOTA.
We do not match P10 under the closest paper-style CV result.

our_protocol: COUGHVID internal baselines and Coswara-to-COUGHVID external transfer
our_metric: COUGHVID internal MFCC AUROC 0.781; external transfer AUROC 0.543
verdict: Internal COUGHVID is lower and external transfer is far lower; external transfer is stricter than their per-dataset internal evaluation.

paper_protocol: HST on Cambridge/COUGHVID tasks, spectrogram transformer
paper_metric: Over 90% AUC reported for COVID detection
our_protocol: Feature-selection + classical model bank, temporal/external stress
verdict: Architecture/protocol mismatch; HST is an advanced architecture reference, not directly comparable to our conservative external result.

our_protocol: Audio-only main model plus symptoms as confounding audit
our_metric: Symptoms-only metadata AUROC 0.932; final audio/multimodal internal AUROC 0.897
verdict: Not comparable because symptoms are used as predictive prior in P8 and as audit/confounder in ours.

paper_protocol: Nested leave-p-out, MFCC/SFS/CNN-LSTM-ResNet and Sarcos validation
paper_metric: Coswara AUC 0.98; Sarcos after SFS about 0.94
our_protocol: Coswara ComParE+IS10 and COUGHVID external transfer
our_metric: CV cough AUROC 0.819; external COUGHVID AUROC 0.543
verdict: Different data construction, validation unit, and external dataset; not a fair direct scoreboard comparison.
